_schema.doc: Behavior fixtures consumed by the /e2e-test skill (converter/.claude/skills/e2e-test/SKILL.md). Each fixture runs in a Roblox Studio Play session against the assembled converted_place.rbxlx. The harness prepends `preamble` to every setup_luau and assert_luau body so fixtures stay terse. The preamble exposes: plr/char/hrp/hum/cam aliases; _G._state (persistent across execute_luau calls); _findVisible (first non-transparent BasePart of a Model — skips the invisible PickupTouchDetector trigger that SimpleFPS attaches to pickup roots); _trackHealth/_minHealth (Humanoid health logging that survives auto-regen); _reset (Codex finding #5: fixture isolation — clears _G._state connections + workspace E2E attributes before each fixture); _pumpMouse(dx, dy) (E2E mouse channel — see docs/E2E_INPUT_CHANNEL.md).
_schema.preamble: local Players = game:GetService('Players'); local plr = Players.LocalPlayer; local char = plr.Character or plr.CharacterAdded:Wait(); local hrp = char:WaitForChild('HumanoidRootPart'); local hum = char:WaitForChild('Humanoid'); local cam = workspace.CurrentCamera; _G._state = _G._state or {}; local function _findVisible(node) if not node then return nil end; if node:IsA('BasePart') then return node end; if node:IsA('Model') and node.PrimaryPart and node.PrimaryPart.Transparency < 0.99 then return node.PrimaryPart end; for _, c in ipairs(node:GetDescendants()) do if c:IsA('BasePart') and c.Transparency < 0.99 then return c end end; return node:FindFirstChildWhichIsA('BasePart', true) end; local function _findHazard(name) for _, x in ipairs(workspace:GetDescendants()) do if x.Name == name and (x:IsA('BasePart') or x:IsA('Model')) then local p = _findVisible(x); if p then return p end end end; return nil end; local function _trackHealth() _G._state.h0 = hum.Health; _G._state.health_log = {}; if _G._state.health_conn then _G._state.health_conn:Disconnect() end; _G._state.health_conn = hum:GetPropertyChangedSignal('Health'):Connect(function() table.insert(_G._state.health_log, hum.Health) end) end; local function _minHealth() local m = _G._state.h0; for _, h in ipairs(_G._state.health_log or {}) do if h < m then m = h end end; return m end; local function _reset() for _, k in ipairs({'health_conn','ammo_conn','snd_conn'}) do local c = _G._state[k]; if c and typeof(c) == 'RBXScriptConnection' then c:Disconnect() end end; for _, c in ipairs(_G._state.conns or {}) do if typeof(c) == 'RBXScriptConnection' then c:Disconnect() end end; _G._state = { conns = {} }; workspace:SetAttribute('E2EMouseDeltaX', 0); workspace:SetAttribute('E2EMouseDeltaY', 0) end; local function _pumpMouse(dx, dy) workspace:SetAttribute('E2EMouseDeltaX', dx); workspace:SetAttribute('E2EMouseDeltaY', dy); workspace:SetAttribute('E2EMouseSeq', (workspace:GetAttribute('E2EMouseSeq') or 0) + 1) end

input_sequence_kinds.mouse_move_note: MCP's user_mouse_input synthesises Delta=(0,0), so do NOT use it for camera yaw/pitch. Mouse-look fixtures use the E2E channel — call _pumpMouse(dx, dy) inside setup_luau. See docs/E2E_INPUT_CHANNEL.md.

_schema.isolation: Every fixture's setup_luau MUST start with '_reset();' (Codex finding #5). Without it, prior connections leak (health_log keeps growing, mouse seq carries over, etc.) and the suite becomes order-dependent. _reset() is idempotent and cheap; if a fixture genuinely depends on the previous fixture's state, declare it via depends_on but still call _reset() and reconstruct what you need.
_schema.dry_run_notes_2026_05_21: Pre-PR-B1 dry-run: fixtures 1, 2a-d, 4a-b, 5, 6a, 7a-b, 8, 9 PASS against output/SimpleFPS_run_2026-05-19/converted_place.rbxlx. Fixture 6b (rifle viewport) SOFT-FAILS — converter doesn't spawn a camera-attached viewmodel yet; tracked in docs/UNSUPPORTED.md. Mouse fixtures 3a/3b used the deleted _G._mockMouseDelta seam; this PR rewrites them to use the workspace E2E channel — the channel is read by scaffolding/fps.py and the AI prompt (see docs/E2E_INPUT_CHANNEL.md). Detection patterns learned from dry-run: AmmoUpdate BindableEvent is the cleanest shoot signal (RemoteEvent.OnClientEvent requires server-side OnServerEvent which execute_luau can't bind); shoot sounds are created fresh per shot via Instance.new('Sound').Parent=rootPart so DescendantAdded catches them; Humanoid auto-regen erases damage in ~10s so use a property-changed log + min(), not end-state comparison.

fixtures.feature: 1. After spawn, I do not drop in the water — stay on the dock.
fixtures.assert_luau: return hum.Health > 0 and hum.FloorMaterial ~= Enum.Material.Air and hum.FloorMaterial ~= Enum.Material.Water

fixtures.feature: 2. The W key moves the player forward (camera-relative).
2. The S key moves the player backward.

3. Mouse motion yaws the camera. Uses the workspace E2E mouse channel (see docs/E2E_INPUT_CHANNEL.md) — MCP synthesises Delta=(0,0) for user_mouse_input so this channel is the only working path. _pumpMouse bumps E2EMouseSeq + sets DeltaX/Y; the client (scaffolding/fps.py, or AI-transpiled Player.luau via the fps_e2e_mouse_channel coherence pack) reads the channel additively for one frame and acks via E2EMouseAckSeq.
3. Vertical mouse motion pitches the camera. Same channel as mouse_yaw_rotates_camera; tests Y axis.
4a. The card key has visible animation before pickup. The visible part is the 'Key' MeshPart under the KeyPickup model — _findVisible skips the invisible PickupTouchDetector trigger.
fixtures.assert_luau: if not _G._state.p then return false end; local moved = (_G._state.p.Position - _G._state.cf0.Position).Magnitude > 0.05; local rotated = (_G._state.p.CFrame.RightVector - _G._state.cf0.RightVector).Magnitude > 0.05; return moved or rotated

fixtures.feature: 4b. Walking onto the card key picks it up. Pickup is signaled by char:GetAttribute('hasKey') == true (see Door.luau:83).
fixtures.setup_luau: _reset(); local k = workspace:FindFirstChild('KeyPickup', true); local touch = (k and (k:FindFirstChild('PickupTouchDetector') or _findVisible(k))); hrp.CFrame = CFrame.new(touch.Position + Vector3.new(0, 3, 0))

fixtures.feature: 5. With the card key held, walking up to the nearest door opens it. The nearest Door (by world distance) is selected dynamically — Beach.Door in the current SimpleFPS scene. The visible/animated child under the Door model is named 'door' (lowercase).
fixtures.setup_luau: _reset(); local nearest, bestDist = nil, math.huge; for _, x in ipairs(workspace:GetDescendants()) do if x.Name == 'Door' and x:IsA('Model') then local pp = _findVisible(x); if pp then local d = (pp.Position - hrp.Position).Magnitude; if d < bestDist then nearest = x; bestDist = d end end end end; local door_mesh = nearest and (nearest:FindFirstChild('door') or _findVisible(nearest)); _G._state.p = door_mesh; _G._state.cf0 = door_mesh.CFrame; hrp.CFrame = CFrame.new(door_mesh.Position - door_mesh.CFrame.LookVector * 6 + Vector3.new(0, 2, 0))
fixtures.assert_luau: local p = _G._state.p; if p:GetAttribute('open') == true then return true end; local dPos = (p.Position - _G._state.cf0.Position).Magnitude; local dRot = (p.CFrame.LookVector - _G._state.cf0.LookVector).Magnitude; return dPos > 1 or dRot > 0.2

fixtures.feature: 6a. Walking onto the rifle pickup sets char:GetAttribute('hasRifle') == true. The converter currently does NOT spawn a viewmodel under the character or camera — that's a separate gap tracked in docs/UNSUPPORTED.md. This fixture asserts the gameplay-state outcome only.
fixtures.setup_luau: _reset(); local r = workspace:FindFirstChild('RiflePickup', true); local touch = r and (r:FindFirstChild('PickupTouchDetector') or _findVisible(r)); hrp.CFrame = CFrame.new(touch.Position + Vector3.new(0, 3, 0))

fixtures.feature: 6b. The rifle is visible in the player's field of view. SOFT-EXPECTED-FAIL today: no viewmodel exists in the current converted SimpleFPS output. When the converter adds a camera-attached viewmodel (or character-attached weapon model) this fixture should start passing without modification.
fixtures.assert_luau: local r = char:FindFirstChild('Rifle', true) or char:FindFirstChild('FPS_Weapon_Mount', true) or cam:FindFirstChild('Rifle', true) or cam:FindFirstChild('Viewmodel', true); local tip = r and _findVisible(r); if not tip then return false end; local sp, on = cam:WorldToViewportPoint(tip.Position); return on and sp.Z > 0 and sp.X > 0 and sp.X < cam.ViewportSize.X and sp.Y > 0 and sp.Y < cam.ViewportSize.Y

fixtures.feature: 7a. Left click fires a bullet. Detection: AmmoUpdate BindableEvent fires every time Player.luau's shoot() decrements ammo (Player.luau:288 AmmoUpdate:Fire(curAmmo)). Hooking RemoteEvent.OnClientEvent doesn't work because PlayerShoot uses FireServer which only OnServerEvent receives — and execute_luau runs client-side so it can't bind server events.
fixtures.setup_luau: _reset(); _G._state.ammo_events = {}; local au = game:GetService('ReplicatedStorage'):FindFirstChild('AmmoUpdate'); if au then _G._state.ammo_conn = au.Event:Connect(function(amt) table.insert(_G._state.ammo_events, amt) end) end

fixtures.feature: 7b. Shooting plays a sound. Detection is implementation-agnostic: we hook Sound.Played on every existing Sound under the character + camera AND on any Sound added after the click. The original C# created a fresh Instance.new('Sound') per shot; the AI-transpiled Player.luau instead replays a pre-existing HumanoidRootPart.Sound — both fire .Played, so counting plays catches either. (Verified live 2026-05-22: shooting fires HumanoidRootPart.Sound.Played.)
fixtures.setup_luau: _reset(); _G._state.plays = 0; local function _hook(s) if s:IsA('Sound') then table.insert(_G._state.conns, s.Played:Connect(function() _G._state.plays = _G._state.plays + 1 end)) end end; for _, s in ipairs(char:GetDescendants()) do _hook(s) end; for _, s in ipairs(cam:GetDescendants()) do _hook(s) end; table.insert(_G._state.conns, char.DescendantAdded:Connect(function(inst) _hook(inst) end))

fixtures.feature: 8. Walking to a mine triggers damage. Mine is a MeshPart at workspace.DynamicObjects.Level.Beach.BeachTurrets.Mine (no enclosing Model — _findVisible handles BasePart directly). The Humanoid auto-regen erases damage within ~10s, so we track Health via GetPropertyChangedSignal and assert min() < h0 rather than end-state.

9. The turret pair identifies the player and damages them. Discovery now uses `_ScriptClass=="Turret"` (generic mode) and matches any numbered slot (`_ScriptClass`, `_ScriptClass_1`, `_ScriptClass_2`, ...) since the converter emits per-MonoBehaviour-index slots at `scene_converter.py:3113-3129`, with a legacy `<child Script named Turret>` fallback. The 285-stud-wide Collider child is what receives Touched; teleporting 15 studs in front + 3 up of the visible part puts the player inside it. KNOWN CONVERTER GAP (as of 2026-05-24): TurretBullet damage path is broken because runtime-spawned prefab clones lack `_SceneRuntimeId`, so the TurretBullet component's `self.gameObject` is nil and Touched/ApplyImpulse silently no-op (warning: `[scene_runtime] connectGameObjectSignal: no touch part on nil`). Tracked in PR #145 (https://github.com/ntornow/unity2rbxlx/pull/145 — template SRI stamping in `prefab_packages.py`). This fixture will continue to fail at the damage assertion (not at setup) until that PR lands.
fixtures.setup_luau: _reset(); _trackHealth(); local turrets = {}; local function _hasTurretClass(x) if x:GetAttribute('_ScriptClass') == 'Turret' then return true end; for k, v in pairs(x:GetAttributes()) do if v == 'Turret' and string.match(k, '^_ScriptClass') then return true end end; return false end; for _, x in ipairs(workspace:GetDescendants()) do if x.Name == 'Turret' and x:IsA('Model') and _hasTurretClass(x) then table.insert(turrets, x) end end; if #turrets == 0 then for _, x in ipairs(workspace:GetDescendants()) do if x.Name == 'Turret' and x:IsA('Model') then local s = x:FindFirstChild('Turret'); if s and s:IsA('Script') then table.insert(turrets, x) end end end end; _G._state.t = turrets[1]; local p = _findVisible(_G._state.t); hrp.CFrame = CFrame.new(p.Position + p.CFrame.LookVector * 15 + Vector3.new(0, 3, 0))
fixtures.assert_luau: local p = _findVisible(_G._state.t); local hrp_pos = char.Parent and hrp.Position or nil; local aimed = true; if hrp_pos then local dirToMe = (hrp_pos - p.Position).Unit; aimed = p.CFrame.LookVector:Dot(dirToMe) > 0.6 end; local damaged = (char.Parent == nil) or _minHealth() < _G._state.h0; return aimed and damaged

fixtures.feature: 10. Console health: across the entire play session (load + spawn + every prior fixture's inputs), LogService:GetLogHistory() contains zero error-level entries. Catches converter-output bugs that surface as runtime errors — module init throws, nil dereferences in input handlers, undefined globals, type errors — independently of whether any single gameplay assertion catches them. Filters to Enum.MessageType.MessageError only (warnings and info are ignored); _reset() doesn't touch LogService, so the log is the full session from Play start. Placed last so it sees every prior fixture's effects. If any earlier fixture failure was caused by an error, that error will appear here too — two independent signals for the same bug is fine.
fixtures.assert_luau: local errs = {}; for _, entry in ipairs(game:GetService('LogService'):GetLogHistory()) do if entry.messageType == Enum.MessageType.MessageError then table.insert(errs, entry.message) end end; _G._state.console_errors = errs; return #errs == 0
